name: New Potatoes with Salami & Sorrel
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop the onion roughly and partially soften it in 2 generous tablespoons of olive oil. Do not let it brown.
2. Scrub the potatoes (but do not peel them) and steam them.
3. Wash and dry the sorrel, discarding tough stalks. Roll up the leaves tightly in your fingers, just a few at a time, as though making a cigarette, and snip across into fine ribbons.
4. Tip the softened onion and its juices into a large bowl.
5. Stir in ½ teaspoon white wine vinegar, ½ teaspoon wholegrain mustard, salt, pepper, and freshly toasted and ground coriander seed.
6. Add the potatoes to the bowl while still piping hot, so they absorb the flavors of the dressing. Cut each potato in half or into quarters depending on size, and toss gently.
7. Scatter some of the sorrel over the base of a shallow serving dish.
8. Pile the potatoes into the center, encircle them with the sliced salami, and scatter the remaining ribbons of sorrel over the top. Serve while the potatoes are still warm.
